cognitive_passport._description: Based on Bouman, Steg & Kiers (2018, Frontiers in Psychology). Hedonic value orientation: concern for personal pleasure, enjoyment, and comfort in the present moment. These individuals prioritise how travel feels — whether it is comfortable, enjoyable, and stress-free. They resist effort, inconvenience, and discomfort. Steg et al. (2014) showed hedonic values are important for understanding environmental behaviour independently of egoistic values. E-SVS hedonic items: pleasure, enjoying life, self-indulgent. Hedonic values sit on the self-enhancement side of Schwartz's circumplex, meaning they compete with biospheric and altruistic values.

_value_rationale.pro_environment: Very low — hedonic values negatively predict pro-environmental beliefs and behaviours (Bouman et al. 2018; Steg et al. 2014). Sustainable options are often perceived as less comfortable or enjoyable
_value_rationale.physical_activity: Lowest priority — physical effort is the opposite of comfort and pleasure; walking or cycling perceived as effortful and unenjoyable
_value_rationale.privacy: Moderate-high — crowded PT reduces comfort and enjoyment; preference for personal space when possible
_value_rationale.autonomy: Moderate — values freedom to choose when and how to travel for maximum enjoyment, but less extreme than egoistic type
_value_rationale.hedonism: Supreme importance — the defining characteristic of this orientation. Travel should be pleasant, enjoyable, and stress-free (Steg et al. 2014 introduced hedonic values as a fourth distinct value type)
_value_rationale.cost_saving: Low-moderate — cost is less important than experience quality; will pay more for a comfortable or enjoyable journey
_value_rationale.speed: High — slow journeys are unpleasant; speed reduces time spent in an uncomfortable state
_value_rationale.safety: Moderate-high — safety contributes to feeling secure and comfortable during travel
_value_rationale.comfort: Highest alongside hedonism — climate-controlled, seated, smooth travel is essential; this directly expresses the hedonic orientation
